row085_contract.title: Actor, object, body-region, clothing, and surface segmentation/tracking
row085_contract.completion_criteria: Persistent ownership tracks have measured identity-switch, occlusion, reappearance, and lost-track behavior.

source_authority.authority: Row085 persistent ownership, occlusion, reappearance, and measured failure acceptance
actor/object track fusion and fail-closed contact ownership architecture
actor/object/contact role and timing preservation boundary
visible, clothed, occluded, and multi-actor ownership QA cases

dependency_authority.row085_effect: Persistent frame-to-frame ownership cannot be certified without an accepted canonical frame, PTS, cut, and missing-frame authority.

useful_shape: character instance and immutable package binding
provider person index and render order
bounding box, silhouette mask, pose, and depth references
identity, wardrobe, surface, and contact references
missing_or_underconstrained_authority: no actor, object, limb, hand, foot, clothing, prop, furniture, or surface entity taxonomy
no unique or monotonic frame-index invariant
no identity-switch count, rate, cause, or threshold
no lost-track state, duration, cause, or threshold
no occlusion-gap start, end, duration, or threshold
no reappearance association decision or score
no false-positive, false-negative, fragmentation, merge, or split metric
no ground-truth annotation or benchmark reference
no confidence calibration or certification ceiling
owner_never_changes is asserted as a constant rather than demonstrated by measured observations

video_frame_manifest_schema_assessment.useful_boundary: The frame manifest can point to owner-bound spatial observations and confidence per frame.
row085_gaps: no requirement that a track ID is unique inside a frame
no cross-frame persistence or owner-consistency check
no occlusion or reappearance state machine
no switch, loss, gap, fragmentation, merge, or split measurement

legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.credit: static character-to-region and mask ownership mapping
legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.limitation: no frame, time, track, occlusion, reappearance, or metric fields
single artifact record with no temporal continuity authority

legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.credit: owner, region, mask, and optional ownership confidence binding
legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.limitation: no persistent track reference or fail-closed unsupported-ownership state

legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.credit: binary occlusion and contact-shadow QA flags
legacy_region_and_contact_schema_assessm.limitation: no track, gap, duration, reappearance, or ownership identity measurement

adversarial_schema_probe.interpretation: The schema accepts duplicate frame indices, decreasing PTS, and a fully invisible two-sample track while the closed shape rejects direct measurement fields required by Row085. Constants claiming complete coverage and unchanged ownership do not replace measured evidence.

focused_local_test_result.scope_interpretation: The complete third-pass contract suite proves schema construction and package-level planning invariants. It has no temporal-track example and does not execute or benchmark Row085 ownership behavior.

required_tracking_contract_missing: canonical Row084 timeline and cut-epoch input binding
typed entity taxonomy covering actors, body regions, clothing, props, furniture, and surfaces
persistent owner and track identifier lifecycle
detector, segmenter, tracker, association model, revision, and parameter identities
per-frame detection, mask, pose, depth, confidence, and provenance
active, occluded, lost, reidentified, terminated, split, and merged states
occluder and depth-order evidence with gap start, end, and duration
reappearance candidate set, association score, decision, and rejection reason
ground-truth annotation and held-out benchmark fixture references
identity-switch, fragmentation, merge, split, false-positive, false-negative, and lost-track metrics
unsupported or ambiguous ownership certification ceiling
content-addressed track manifest and deterministic replay receipt
visual overlay review binding tracks, masks, IDs, occlusion states, and contact events
acceptance_matrix_required: single actor enters, remains visible, and exits without an identity switch
two visually similar actors cross and separate without owner exchange
partial and full occlusion retain or explicitly lose ownership
reappearance inside and outside the allowed temporal and spatial gate
long occlusion expires a track according to registered policy
camera motion, cut, and missing-frame gaps do not fabricate identity continuity
limb, hand, and foot tracks remain attached to the correct actor
clothing regions remain attached through pose and visibility changes
prop handoff changes ownership explicitly rather than silently
furniture and static surfaces do not merge with actors or props
mask split and merge events are recorded and measured
duplicate detections and false positives are rejected or measured
identity-switch, lost-track, gap, and reappearance metrics match annotated truth
threshold-edge and low-confidence cases fail closed
tampered track, mask, ownership, and benchmark evidence is rejected
deterministic replay preserves track IDs, states, metrics, and hashes
combined frame, track-overlay, contact, and audio review passes on genuine media

test_and_runtime_boundary.ec2_not_invoked_reason: The prerequisite Row084 authority and local implementation, schema-semantic, metric, benchmark, and runtime-receipt gates already fail; GPU execution cannot cure or responsibly bypass those authority gaps.

scheduler_and_worker_containment.containment_note: The signed intent was deferred with no routing or wake. Existing paused automations, disabled worker tasks, disabled Mask maintenance tasks, worker snapshots, FLUX2 paths, portfolio paths, and unrelated dirty files were not modified.

hold_decision.reason: Row084 remains incomplete. The project has useful closed per-frame and temporal track schemas, older static owner/region/mask contracts, declared blocking metrics, and a 37-test passing planning-contract suite. However, there is no production actor/object/body-region tracker, persistent ownership state machine, complete entity taxonomy, measured identity-switch/lost-track/occlusion-gap/reappearance behavior, annotated benchmark, deterministic replay, unsupported-ownership certification block, direct runtime receipt, or combined track-overlay/contact/audio review. The temporal schema accepts duplicate frame indices, decreasing PTS, and fully invisible samples while rejecting the measurement fields required by Row085.
hold_decision.safe_next_action: After Row084 is accepted, freeze a Row085 track lifecycle contract and entity taxonomy that binds the canonical timeline, detector/segmenter/association identities, per-frame evidence, owner states, occlusion gaps, reappearance decisions, metric definitions, thresholds, certification ceilings, and content hashes. Implement one deterministic tracker and evaluator; validate annotated single-actor, crossing, occlusion, reappearance, loss, handoff, split/merge, camera/cut/gap, tamper, and replay fixtures; then run genuine media overlays with contact/audio review and emit the tracker-declared runtime evidence. Continue independent Codex PM/QA work meanwhile.
